rulings.Case Title: IVAN MERL BARNEY VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Calaveras Asbestos Ltd.S Written Discovery And To Deem Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.S Requests For Admissions Admitted And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, November 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Calaveras Asbestos Ltd.¿s motion to compel responses to its written discovery, to deem its requests for admissions admitted, and request for sanctions of $1687.50 is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs have 10 days to serve verified responses, without objections, to defendant¿s special interrogatories, form interrogatories and requests for production. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY RUPRECHT, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses; To Deem Admissions Admitted; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, November 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rapid American Corporation¿s motion to compel discovery responses, to deem admissions admitted and for sanctions is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed November 20. = (503/TLJ)
